stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI topics including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the ongoing community interest and availability of major LLMs like Llama 3.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text generation within the AI/ML community.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: OpenAI files for IPO, following Anthropic
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: OpenAI has taken a major step toward an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by filing a Form S-1 with the SEC.
This filing places OpenAI in direct competition with its rival, Anthropic, which filed an S-1 earlier in June.
The news signals a significant maturation and potential shift in the financial landscape of the leading AI companies.

stories.title: Tests suggest Russian satellites can jam GPS on a continental scale
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Tests indicate Russian satellites possess the capability to jam GPS signals across a continental scale.
The reported GPS interference across Europe has raised significant geopolitical questions regarding Russian intent.
The story highlights a critical vulnerability in global navigation systems due to potential state-level electronic warfare capabilities.

stories.title: macOS 27 requires Apple Silicon, as Apple draws down the Intel Mac era
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: macOS 27 adoption mandates Apple Silicon (M1 or newer).
This signals the definitive end of support for Intel-based Macs.
It represents a major, ongoing industry shift toward Apple's proprietary silicon ecosystem.

stories.title: Meta alleges NSO violated spyware injunction with new WhatsApp attacks
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Meta (WhatsApp) claims NSO Group violated a spyware injunction.
The alleged violation involves new attacks targeting WhatsApp.
WhatsApp is seeking a court order to hold NSO in contempt.

stories.title: For the 2nd time in weeks, Microsoft packages laced with credential stealer
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a recurring security vulnerability involving credential stealer packages.
The malicious packages are designed to execute self-replicating code immediately upon being opened by an AI agent.
This highlights a significant and growing security risk in the integration of AI agents with software packages.

stories.title: Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP)
stories.author: Philip Black
bullet_points: Foundation models are evolving beyond simple response generation into complex operational roles.
Future AI systems will involve multi-agent and multi-human collaborations, handling tasks from code to clinical decisions.
The technical challenge lies in managing collaboration across different teams, time zones, and trust boundaries.

stories.title: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
stories.author: raffael_de
bullet_points: Microsoft's open-source tools were compromised in a security breach.
The attack specifically targeted and resulted in the theft of passwords belonging to AI developers.
This highlights significant security vulnerabilities within the open-source ecosystem used by AI professionals.

stories.title: Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models
stories.author: unclefuzzy
bullet_points: Apple is integrating AI capabilities by building new architecture around Google Gemini models.
This signals a significant strategic partnership between Apple and Google in the AI space.
The development points towards a major industry shift in how major tech players are adopting large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating a focus on large language model performance.
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies, including transformers and PyTorch.
The tags indicate the model's use in text-generation pipelines and utilizes modern ML formats like safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Apple is using AI to fix Safari’s extension problem
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple is leveraging AI to address a long-standing weakness in Safari: its limited extension ecosystem.
The new approach allows users to 'vibe-code' their own extensions, potentially lowering the barrier to entry.
This move signals a shift in Apple's development philosophy for Safari, moving away from strictly stringent requirements.

stories.title: Say hi to "Siri AI"—Apple announces new, more "conversational" voice assistant
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Apple is introducing a new, more 'conversational' voice assistant, dubbed 'Siri AI'.
The update is scheduled for release this fall.
The overhaul involves a two-tiered AI model powered by Google.

stories.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
stories.author: Ruslan Salakhutdinov
bullet_points: Introduces iOSWorld, a new benchmark designed to test 'personally intelligent' phone agents.
The benchmark simulates a persistent user identity across 26 native iOS apps, unlike current impersonal benchmarks.
Focuses on agents that can reason over a user's actual device data (history, preferences, transactions) rather than isolated instructions.

stories.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
stories.author: Ruslan Salakhutdinov
bullet_points: Introduces iOSWorld, a novel benchmark designed to test the capabilities of 'personally intelligent' phone agents.
Addresses a gap in existing mobile agent benchmarks by focusing on personalization, requiring agents to reason over persistent user identity and history.
The benchmark utilizes a native iOS simulator spanning 26 interconnected apps containing real-world user data (e.g., transactions, messages).

stories.title: I Tested Nex-N2-Pro — A Free Open-Source Model That's Matching GPT-5.5 on Coding Benchmarks
stories.author: divyesh5981
bullet_points: Nex-N2-Pro is a new, free, open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model.
The model boasts a large parameter count (397B total, 17B active) and claims performance comparable to GPT-5.5 on coding benchmarks.
Its open-source nature makes it highly relevant for the AI community and potential industry adoption.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these personal AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Apple’s new parental controls are for keeping Apple out of trouble
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Apple introduced new parental controls at WWDC, officially aimed at improving child safety and managing screen time.
The updates are interpreted as a defensive strategy by Apple in the context of growing global internet regulation debates.
The move suggests a potential industry conflict between Apple and other major app developers like Meta regarding content control.

stories.title: Apple drops support for a long list of Apple Watches with latest OS updates
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Apple is discontinuing software support for several older Apple Watch models with the latest OS updates.
The removal of support affects at least three generations of Apple Watches.
This trend highlights Apple's pattern of phasing out older hardware with new software releases.

stories.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The FCC has granted a waiver concerning Amazon's Leo satellite broadband constellation.
This action allows Amazon to proceed with its second large satellite broadband constellation.
The FCC justified the waiver by citing the promotion of public interest through increased broadband availability.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google NotebookLM is receiving a significant upgrade.
The update integrates Gemini 3.5 and 'Antigravity' features.
Access to these new capabilities is currently restricted to AI Ultra and enterprise accounts.

stories.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
stories.author: Xiaojuan Qi
bullet_points: Introduces OmniGameArena, a new unified benchmark designed for evaluating Vision-Language Model (VLM) agents in game environments.
Addresses limitations in existing benchmarks, such as relying on single first-attempt scores and focusing only on solo play.
Provides a standardized platform to compare heterogeneous agent classes (commercial, open-weight, and specialized policies) across twelve new Unreal Engine 5 games.

stories.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
stories.author: Yue Wu
bullet_points: PTL-Diffusion introduces a novel diffusion model approach addressing limitations of standard models.
It moves beyond the standard single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution.
The model incorporates 'Manifold-Aware' techniques to better capture low-dimensional structure in data.

stories.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
stories.author: Irene Solaiman
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inconsistency in how AI evaluation results are reported across various platforms (leaderboards, model cards, etc.).
It proposes 'Evaluation Cards' as an interpretive layer to standardize and improve the traceability of AI evaluation data.
The core problem identified is the difficulty readers face in reliably comparing results or tracing aggregate claims to their underlying evidence.

stories.title: Topological Neural Operators
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: Introduces Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) to extend Neural Operators beyond point/edge data.
TNOs model data on cell complexes, representing features across varying dimensions.
Utilizes Discrete Exterior Calculus to enable explicit cross-dimensional coupling (gradient, curl, divergence).

stories.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
stories.author: Ladan Tahvildari
bullet_points: Addresses the reliability issues (LLM hallucinations, error propagation) in multi-agent code generation systems.
Introduces Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy (FASE) to quantify uncertainty in code generation.
Aims to overcome the computational cost associated with traditional LLM-driven equivalence checks for semantic uncertainty.

stories.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
stories.author: Yifan Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in AI safety: current methods rely on downstream 'hard safety filters' to enforce constraints.
The authors argue that these filters can mask poor policy learning, meaning success might be attributed to the filter rather than the policy itself.
The research proposes a new framework to determine 'who earns the safety'—the learned policy or its protective layers—to improve safety attribution.

stories.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of integrating general-purpose coding agents with specialized scientific simulators.
It frames simulator setup as an 'agent-tool interface grounding' problem, minimizing required adaptations.
The goal is to allow off-the-shelf coding agents to configure and run complex scientific software without extensive domain expertise.

stories.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
stories.author: Tianyu Pang
bullet_points: The paper addresses challenges in Reinforcement Learning (RL) applied to Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically concerning off-policy training.
It critiques existing methods (like PPO/GRPO) for using ratio-clipping, arguing that the importance ratio is insufficient for capturing distributional shift in large vocabularies.
The research focuses on improving the stability and effectiveness of trust-region control mechanisms within LLM RL.

stories.title: Topological Neural Operators
stories.author: Tolga Birdal
bullet_points: Introduces Topological Neural Operators (TNOs) for operator learning on cell complexes.
Extends Neural Operators (NOs) from point/edge functions to topological domains.
Utilizes Discrete Exterior Calculus to model interactions and enable cross-dimensional coupling (gradient, curl, divergence).

stories.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models
stories.author: Nan Duan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Echo-Memory,' a study focusing on memory mechanisms within action-conditioned world models.
The models generate multi-segment videos based on initial frames, text prompts, and camera-action sequences.
A key challenge addressed is the failure of scene consistency (objects changing silently) when the camera moves away and returns.

stories.title: Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration: Automating Time-Series Models for the Cloud-Edge Continuum
stories.author: Raymond Knopp
bullet_points: Addresses the 'cold start' problem in orchestrating time-series models across the Cloud-Edge Continuum (CEC).
Proposes a fully automated solution to enable Zero Touch Predictive Orchestration for volatile edge environments.
Focuses on overcoming the limitations of both localized (data-starved) and generalized (non-specific) predictive models.

stories.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
stories.author: Martin Schrimpf
bullet_points: Introduces 'Topo-Omni,' a new deep topographic multimodal model for neuroscience.
Addresses limitations of previous unimodal models by integrating visual, auditory, and language data.
Aims to map the contiguous and integrated processing streams across different cortical regions.

stories.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
stories.author: Arjun Mukherjee
bullet_points: Addresses the critical challenge of data scarcity in Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for low-resource Indigenous languages.
Proposes a novel data synthesis methodology, using community-sourced dictionaries to create a synthetic corpus, avoiding problematic web-scraping.
Applies Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) via LoRA adapters on mT5 to bootstrap models for Q'eqchi' Mayan.

stories.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of grounding general-purpose coding agents to specialized scientific simulation software.
Proposes 'SIGA,' a framework for creating minimal, self-evolving adapters to bridge the gap between agents and simulator input languages.
Focuses on reducing the significant time domain scientists currently spend learning complex simulator configurations.

stories.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
stories.author: Arjun Mukherjee
bullet_points: Addresses the critical challenge of data scarcity in Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for low-resource Indigenous languages, specifically Q'eqchi' Mayan.
Proposes a novel data synthesis methodology to create large parallel corpora from community-sourced dictionaries, bypassing problematic web-scraping.
Employs Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using LoRA adapters on mT5 to efficiently train the NMT model.

stories.title: Multi-Turn Evaluation of Deep Research Agents Under Process-Level Feedback
stories.author: Jeff Z. Pan
bullet_points: The research addresses a gap in current benchmarks for Deep Research Agents (DRAs) by moving beyond single-shot output evaluation.
It introduces a multi-turn evaluation framework to test how DRAs improve their reports based on feedback.
The study compares two feedback mechanisms: self-reflection (internal revision) and process-level feedback (external guidance on research strategy).

stories.title: The Neutral Mask: How RLHF Provides Shallow Alignment while Leaving Partisan Structure Intact in a Large Language Model
stories.author: Wendy K. Tam
bullet_points: The paper investigates the limitations of Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) in achieving deep alignment in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It posits that RLHF may only result in 'functional compliance' rather than true alignment with 'human values.'
The research offers a mechanistic analysis into how RLHF might preserve underlying partisan structures despite alignment efforts.

stories.title: IS-CoT: Breaking the Long-form Generation Collapse via Interleaved Structural Thinking
stories.author: Min Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the 'long-form generation collapse' in LLMs, where performance degrades significantly beyond short texts.
Proposes 'IS-CoT' (Interleaved Structural Thinking) as a method to improve coherence and controllability in long-form writing.
Identifies the limitation of static hierarchical planning as the root cause of the performance degradation in extended generation.

stories.title: Learning to Attack and Defend: Adaptive Red Teaming of Language Models via GRPO
stories.author: Mark Russinovich
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for adaptive AI red teaming to counter evolving language model attacks.
It proposes AdvGRPO, a novel co-training framework to stabilize GRPO for joint attacker-defender optimization.
The research aims to improve the robustness of language models by enabling continuous, adaptive adversarial training.

stories.title: From 60% to 93%: How We Built a Continuous Evaluation Framework for LLM Systems
stories.author: jamesli
bullet_points: The article details the development of a Continuous Evaluation Framework specifically for LLM systems.
It highlights a significant performance improvement, moving from 60% to 93% in the evaluation process.
It is part of a larger, ongoing technical series focused on building production-grade LLM applications.
